heading: Exchange of real property held for productive use or investment
label: 1031 Exchange of real property held for productive use or investment
notes: Codificationand made identical amendments to this section. The amendments by were repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246

Amendments2017—, substituted “real property” for “property” in section catchline.Pub. L. 115–97, § 13303(b)(5)Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “real property” for “property” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 115–97, § 13303(a)Subsec. (a)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “This subsection shall not apply to any exchange of—Pub. L. 115–97, § 13303(b)(1)(A)“(A) stock in trade or other property held primarily for sale,“(B) stocks, bonds, or notes,“(C) other securities or evidences of indebtedness or interest,“(D) interests in a partnership,“(E) certificates of trust or beneficial interests, or“(F) choses in action.For purposes of this section, an interest in a partnership which has in effect a valid election under section 761(a) to be excluded from the application of all of subchapter K shall be treated as an interest in each of the assets of such partnership and not as an interest in a partnership.”Subsec. (e). , (3), added subsec. (e) and struck out former subsec. (e). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of this section, livestock of different sexes are not property of a like kind.”Pub. L. 115–97, § 13303(b)(2)Subsec. (h). , amended subsec. (h) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (h) related to special rules for foreign real and personal property.Pub. L. 115–97, § 13303(b)(4)Subsec. (i). , struck out subsec. (i). Text read as follows: “For purposes of subsection (a)(2)(B), the term ‘stocks’ shall not include shares in a mutual ditch, reservoir, or irrigation company if at the time of the exchange—Pub. L. 115–97, § 13303(b)(1)(B)“(1) the mutual ditch, reservoir, or irrigation company is an organization described in section 501(c)(12)(A) (determined without regard to the percentage of its income that is collected from its members for the purpose of meeting losses and expenses), and“(2) the shares in such company have been recognized by the highest court of the State in which such company was organized or by applicable State statute as constituting or representing real property or an interest in real property.”2008—Subsec. (i). added subsec. (i).Pub. L. 110–2462005—Subsec. (h)(2)(B). substituted “subparagraphs” for “subparagraph” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 109–1351999—Subsec. (d). , in last sentence, substituted “assumed (as determined under section 357(d)) a liability of the taxpayer” for “assumed a liability of the taxpayer or acquired from the taxpayer property subject to a liability” and struck out “or acquisition (in the amount of the liability)” after “such assumption”.Pub. L. 106–361997—Subsec. (h). amended heading and text of subsec. (h) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of this section, real property located in the United States and real property located outside the United States are not property of a like kind.”Pub. L. 105–341990—Subsec. (a)(2). , inserted at end “For purposes of this section, an interest in a partnership which has in effect a valid election under section 761(a) to be excluded from the application of all of subchapter K shall be treated as an interest in each of the assets of such partnership and not as an interest in a partnership.”Pub. L. 101–508, § 11703(d)(1)Subsec. (f)(3). , substituted “section 267(b) or 707(b)(1)” for “section 267(b)”.Pub. L. 101–508, § 11701(h)1989—Subsecs. (f) to (h). added subsecs. (f) to (h).Pub. L. 101–2391986—Subsec. (a)(3)(A). substituted “on or before the day” for “before the day”.Pub. L. 99–5141984—Subsec. (a). , in amending subsec. generally, designated existing provisions as par. (1), substituted “No gain or loss shall be recognized on the exchange of property held for productive use in a trade or business or for investment if such property is exchanged solely for property of like kind which is to be held either for productive use in a trade or business or for investment” for “No gain or loss shall be recognized if property held for productive use in trade or business or for investment (not including stock in trade or other property held primarily for sale, nor stocks, bonds, notes, choses in action, certificates of trust or beneficial interest, or other securities or evidences of indebtedness or interest) is exchanged solely for property of a like kind to be held either for productive use in trade or business or for investment”, and added pars. (2) and (3).Pub. L. 98–369, § 77(a)1969—Subsec. (e). added subsec. (e).Pub. L. 91–1721959—Subsecs. (b) to (d). inserted references to section 1037(a) in subsecs. (b) and (c) and in first two sentences of subsec. (d).Pub. L. 86–3461958—Subsec. (d). inserted in first sentence a comma between “exchanged” and “decreased” and “or decreased in the amount of loss”, and substituted in second sentence “subsection” for “paragraph”.Pub. L. 85–866

children.heading: Nonrecognition of gain or loss from exchanges solely in kind
children.label: a Nonrecognition of gain or loss from exchanges solely in kind

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No gain or loss shall be recognized on the exchange of real property held for productive use in a trade or business or for investment if such real property is exchanged solely for real property of like kind which is to be held either for productive use in a trade or business or for investment.

children.heading: Exception for real property held for sale
children.label: 2 Exception for real property held for sale
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any exchange of real property held primarily for sale.

children.heading: Requirement that property be identified and that exchange be completed not more than 180 days after transfer of exchanged property
children.label: 3 Requirement that property be identified and that exchange be completed not more than 180 days after transfer of exchanged property
A
children.text: such property is not identified as property to be received in the exchange on or before the day which is 45 days after the date on which the taxpayer transfers the property relinquished in the exchange, or

children.label: i
children.text: the day which is 180 days after the date on which the taxpayer transfers the property relinquished in the exchange, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the due date (determined with regard to extension) for the transferor’s return of the tax imposed by this chapter for the taxable year in which the transfer of the relinquished property occurs.

children.heading: Gain from exchanges not solely in kind
children.label: b Gain from exchanges not solely in kind
children.text: If an exchange would be within the provisions of subsection (a), of section 1035(a), of section 1036(a), or of section 1037(a), if it were not for the fact that the property received in exchange consists not only of property permitted by such provisions to be received without the recognition of gain, but also of other property or money, then the gain, if any, to the recipient shall be recognized, but in an amount not in excess of the sum of such money and the fair market value of such other property.

children.heading: Loss from exchanges not solely in kind
children.label: c Loss from exchanges not solely in kind
children.text: If an exchange would be within the provisions of subsection (a), of section 1035(a), of section 1036(a), or of section 1037(a), if it were not for the fact that the property received in exchange consists not only of property permitted by such provisions to be received without the recognition of gain or loss, but also of other property or money, then no loss from the exchange shall be recognized.

children.heading: Basis
children.label: d Basis
children.text: If property was acquired on an exchange described in this section, section 1035(a), section 1036(a), or section 1037(a), then the basis shall be the same as that of the property exchanged, decreased in the amount of any money received by the taxpayer and increased in the amount of gain or decreased in the amount of loss to the taxpayer that was recognized on such exchange. If the property so acquired consisted in part of the type of property permitted by this section, section 1035(a), section 1036(a), or section 1037(a), to be received without the recognition of gain or loss, and in part of other property, the basis provided in this subsection shall be allocated between the properties (other than money) received, and for the purpose of the allocation there shall be assigned to such other property an amount equivalent to its fair market value at the date of the exchange. For purposes of this section, section 1035(a), and section 1036(a), where as part of the consideration to the taxpayer another party to the exchange assumed (as determined under section 357(d)) a liability of the taxpayer, such assumption shall be considered as money received by the taxpayer on the exchange.

children.heading: Application to certain partnerships
children.label: e Application to certain partnerships
children.text: For purposes of this section, an interest in a partnership which has in effect a valid election under section 761(a) to be excluded from the application of all of subchapter K shall be treated as an interest in each of the assets of such partnership and not as an interest in a partnership.

children.heading: Special rules for exchanges between related persons
children.label: f Special rules for exchanges between related persons
A
children.text: a taxpayer exchanges property with a related person,

children.label: B
children.text: there is nonrecognition of gain or loss to the taxpayer under this section with respect to the exchange of such property (determined without regard to this subsection), and

children.label: i
children.text: the related person disposes of such property, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the taxpayer disposes of the property received in the exchange from the related person which was of like kind to the property transferred by the taxpayer,

children.heading: Certain dispositions not taken into account
children.label: 2 Certain dispositions not taken into account
A
children.text: after the earlier of the death of the taxpayer or the death of the related person,

children.label: B
children.text: in a compulsory or involuntary conversion (within the meaning of section 1033) if the exchange occurred before the threat or imminence of such conversion, or

children.label: C
children.text: with respect to which it is established to the satisfaction of the Secretary that neither the exchange nor such disposition had as one of its principal purposes the avoidance of Federal income tax.

children.heading: Related person
children.label: 3 Related person
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “related person” means any person bearing a relationship to the taxpayer described in section 267(b) or 707(b)(1).

children.heading: Treatment of certain transactions
children.label: 4 Treatment of certain transactions
children.text: This section shall not apply to any exchange which is part of a transaction (or series of transactions) structured to avoid the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Special rule where substantial diminution of risk
children.label: g Special rule where substantial diminution of risk

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If paragraph (2) applies to any property for any period, the running of the period set forth in subsection (f)(1)(C) with respect to such property shall be suspended during such period.

children.label: A
children.text: the holding of a put with respect to such property,

children.label: B
children.text: the holding by another person of a right to acquire such property, or

children.label: C
children.text: a short sale or any other transaction.

children.heading: Special rules for foreign real property
children.label: h Special rules for foreign real property
children.text: Real property located in the United States and real property located outside the United States are not property of a like kind.
